message:
refactor(search-core): use idiomatic Rust patterns in test assertions

Clean up two test files in the search-core crate to use more idiomatic
Rust patterns:

  - metrics.rs: replace `.collect::<Vec<_>>()` followed by
    `assert!(!phases.is_empty())` with a direct `.next().is_some()`
    check. This avoids allocating a Vec just to test that an iterator
    is non-empty, which is both clearer in intent and more efficient.

  - two_tier.rs: replace a match-with-binding destructure on
    SearchPhase::Refined with a let-else pattern (`let ... else {
    panic!(...) }`). This is the idiomatic Rust way to assert an enum
    variant and extract fields simultaneously, reducing nesting and
    making the test's intent more readable.

Both changes are test-only and have no effect on runtime behavior or
public API surface.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
